🎯 Study Design & Problem Formulation: Frame the project as short-term emergency department demand forecasting for operational staffing support. Define patient arrival volume as the primary target and use wait time, admission counts, and peak-period behavior as operational pressure signals.
📥 Data Ingestion & Integration: Download the Kaggle hospital emergency room dataset into Notebook/data/raw and structure timestamped patient encounters into hourly demand records for sequential processing.
🧼 Data Cleaning & Quality Assurance: Validate required source columns, parse admission timestamps, check missing or invalid operational fields, sort records chronologically, and create a continuous hourly timeline with explicit zero-arrival hours.
🔍 Exploratory Time-Series Analysis: Assess the regularized hourly patient-arrival series using visual trend checks, rolling summaries, stationarity tests, STL decomposition, and autocorrelation analysis.
🧱 Feature Engineering & Temporal Embeddi: Create leakage-safe calendar, flu-season, weather, lag, rolling-window, and cyclical time features for 1h, 6h, 24h, and 72h forecasting targets.
⏱️ Chronological Split Strategy: Use strict chronological train, validation, and test splits so future observations never leak into model training or feature construction.
📏 Baseline Model Establishment: Build simple forecasting benchmarks including persistence, seasonal naive forecasts, and moving-average forecasts to establish a performance floor.
🤖 Short-Term Forecasting Model Suite: Train and compare interpretable regression models, a compact SARIMAX comparison, and nonlinear tree-based models such as LightGBM or a scikit-learn fallback for short-term ER patient volume forecasting.
🚦 Model Evaluation & Operational Validat: Evaluate models with RMSE, MAE, zero-safe MAPE, mean bias, underprediction rate, peak-period error, and hour/day error summaries to connect statistical accuracy with staffing risk.
✅ Final Summary & Future Work: Summarize the best model by horizon, key operational risks, limitations, and future extensions such as LSTM modeling, explicit staffing simulation, and medium- or long-term seasonal forecasting.
